fund.name: Bessemer Venture Partners
fund.location: San Francisco, CA / New York, NY

partner.name: Sameer Dholakia
partner.title: Partner
partner.background: Former CEO of SendGrid (acquired $3B), operating executive turned investor

thesis_alignment.summary: Bessemer is the premier cloud infrastructure investor. Their 'State of the Cloud' and 'State of AI' reports set industry benchmarks. Sameer's SendGrid background gives him deep developer platform expertise.
relevant_expertise: Sameer Dholakia led SendGrid from startup to $3B acquisition
Strong follow-on capital for Series B/C

warm_signals: Portfolio overlap with LangChain, W&B founders
Developer community connections through SendGrid alumni

outreach_strategy.hook: Reference Bessemer's AI infrastructure thesis and Sameer's SendGrid background
outreach_strategy.angle: Next generation developer infrastructure for AI-native applications
outreach_strategy.differentiation: Infrastructure that learns and adapts; not just static APIs
outreach_strategy.recommended_subject: AI infrastructure for the post-ChatGPT developer stack

email_draft.subject: AI infrastructure for the post-ChatGPT developer stack
email_draft.body:
Hi Sameer,

I've been following Bessemer's State of AI reports and your insights from the SendGrid journey. The shift you're describing — from AI as a feature to AI as infrastructure — is exactly what we're building.

We're creating the infrastructure layer that learns and adapts, not just static APIs. Think of it as the difference between traditional cloud services and AI-native infrastructure that improves with every interaction.

Given your SendGrid experience building developer platforms that scale, I'd value your perspective on our approach. Would you be open to a brief call to explore if there's a fit with Bessemer's AI infrastructure thesis?

Best,
[Founder Name]

packets.notes: Top priority — Bessemer is the definitive AI infrastructure investor

partner.name: Matt McIlwain
partner.title: Managing Director
partner.background: 20+ years investing in AI/ML, wrote the 'Intelligent App Stack' framework

thesis_alignment.summary: Madrona pioneered the 'Intelligent Applications' thesis. Matt McIlwain has been investing in AI for over a decade and publishes definitive frameworks on AI app architecture.

outreach_strategy.hook: Reference Matt's 'Intelligent App Stack' and Madrona's platform shift history
outreach_strategy.angle: Infrastructure for the next wave of intelligent applications
outreach_strategy.differentiation: AI infrastructure designed for multi-agent orchestration
outreach_strategy.recommended_subject: Multi-agent infrastructure for the Intelligent App Stack

email_draft.subject: Multi-agent infrastructure for the Intelligent App Stack
email_draft.body:
Hi Matt,

Your 'Intelligent App Stack' framework has become essential reading for anyone building AI-native companies. The evolution from cloud to mobile to AI represents the biggest platform shift since the internet — and the infrastructure layer is still being defined.

We're building the infrastructure specifically designed for multi-agent orchestration — the coordination layer that makes intelligent applications truly intelligent.

Given Madrona's track record with platform shifts (cloud, mobile, now AI) and your deep AI investment history, I'd love to get your perspective. Open to a brief call?

Best,
[Founder Name]

packets.notes: High priority — Matt is a thought leader in AI application architecture

fund.name: Amplify Partners
fund.location: Palo Alto, CA / New York, NY

partner.name: Sunil Dhaliwal
partner.title: Managing Partner
partner.background: Investing in developer tools since 2000, track record includes Chef, DataDog, Fastly, Snyk

thesis_alignment.summary: Amplify is a purpose-built developer tools and infrastructure fund. Sunil has been investing in this category for 20+ years with exceptional returns. Recent AI investments in LangChain, Fixie, and Steamship show active deployment.
relevant_expertise: Pure developer tools focus for 20+ years
Track record: DataDog, Fastly, Chef, Snyk
Portfolio community of 100+ dev tool companies

outreach_strategy.hook: Reference Amplify's developer tools heritage and recent AI investments
outreach_strategy.angle: The missing piece in the AI developer workflow
outreach_strategy.differentiation: Developer-first AI infrastructure; built by developers for developers
outreach_strategy.recommended_subject: The missing infrastructure in the AI developer workflow

email_draft.subject: The missing infrastructure in the AI developer workflow
email_draft.body:
Hi Sunil,

Amplify's track record in developer tools speaks for itself — DataDog, Fastly, Chef, Snyk. You've consistently identified the infrastructure layers that developers can't live without.

As AI becomes the primary interface for software, we're seeing a massive gap in the developer workflow. We're building the infrastructure that makes AI-native development as seamless as traditional software development.

Given your recent investments in LangChain and AI infrastructure, this seems right in your wheelhouse. Would love to share what we're building and get your perspective.

Best,
[Founder Name]

packets.notes: High priority — Pure-play developer tools focus, exceptional track record

partner.name: Wen-Wen Lam
partner.title: Partner
partner.background: Leads AI infrastructure and applied AI investments, former Google product leader

thesis_alignment.summary: Google's AI-focused venture fund with direct access to Google AI research and infrastructure. Focuses exclusively on AI-native companies with technical differentiation.
relevant_expertise: Technical team with PhDs in ML/AI
Google Cloud and Vertex AI partnerships

outreach_strategy.hook: Reference Gradient's focus on AI-native companies and Google's infrastructure
outreach_strategy.angle: Multi-cloud AI infrastructure that works across providers
outreach_strategy.differentiation: AI infrastructure that works across cloud providers (multi-cloud native)

email_draft.subject: Multi-cloud AI infrastructure — beyond GCP
email_draft.body:
Hi Wen-Wen,

Gradient's focus on AI-native companies with technical differentiation has produced an impressive portfolio. The access to Google AI research and infrastructure is a unique advantage.

We're building AI infrastructure that takes a different approach — multi-cloud native from day one. While we integrate deeply with GCP, AWS, and Azure, we maintain independence so companies aren't locked into any single provider.

Given Gradient's thesis on AI-native infrastructure and Google's cloud leadership, I'd value your perspective. Open to a brief conversation?

Best,
[Founder Name]

packets.notes: Strong technical fund with Google backing

partner.name: Tomasz Tunguz
partner.title: Founder
partner.background: Former Redpoint partner, renowned SaaS analyst, writes the most-read newsletter in VC

thesis_alignment.summary: Tomasz Tunguz founded Theory Ventures to focus exclusively on AI and data infrastructure. His research and writing are required reading in the SaaS/VC community. Data-driven approach to investment selection.

outreach_strategy.hook: Reference Tomasz's writing on AI infrastructure and the Redpoint AI Index
outreach_strategy.angle: Metrics-driven infrastructure with measurable ROI from day one
outreach_strategy.differentiation: Measurable infrastructure ROI from day one
outreach_strategy.recommended_subject: Infrastructure with measurable ROI — as you wrote about

email_draft.subject: Infrastructure with measurable ROI — as you wrote about
email_draft.body:
Hi Tomasz,

Your newsletter is required reading for anyone in SaaS and infrastructure. Your recent analysis on AI infrastructure economics resonated deeply — the need for measurable ROI from day one.

We're building exactly that: AI infrastructure where every metric is transparent and ROI is measurable from the first deployment. No black boxes, no vanity metrics — just clear infrastructure ROI.

Given Theory's focus on data and AI infrastructure and your emphasis on metrics-driven companies, this seems like a natural fit. Would you be open to a brief call to explore?

Best,
[Founder Name]
